name: Korean Barbecue - Bulgogi
recipeInstructions:
1. Bulgogi, or Bulgalbi, refers to broiled (grilled) beef strips and beef ribs, representing an ancient tradition of cooking on a curved iron hotplate, common in northern China and Mongolia.
2. Today, this is adapted for table service with cone-shaped hotplates placed over tabletop burners for a personalized dining experience.
3. Various meats, including mutton, pork, poultry, offal, and seafood, are marinated using a spicy mixture of soy sauce, sesame oil, garlic, ginger, pepper or chili, toasted sesame seeds, and green onions.
4. The meat is marinated well in advance to ensure a strong flavor.
5. Cooking time is minimal, just enough to cook through and seal the surface.
6. Serve Bulgogi with white rice and yangnyum kanjang sauce, along with an assortment of accompaniments such as kim chee (chili pickled cabbage) and jeot khal (spiced whitefish).
7. Cut the beef across the grain into very thin slices, then cut into narrow strips.
8. In a glass or stainless steel dish, mix all remaining ingredients together thoroughly.
9. Add the beef and stir until well combined.
10. Cover and let marinate for at least 3 hours.
11. Preheat a tabletop broiler (Hibachi or Ghengis Kahn Cooker), placing an asbestos mat or other suitable heat shield on the table to protect it.
12. Each diner, or the host/hostess, places a portion of meat on the broiler and cooks quickly on both sides.
13. Dip the cooked meat into the sauce before eating.
14. Use wooden chopsticks or small forks/fondue forks for serving.
